zh.North Berwick, Haddingtonshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾諾斯 貝維其可
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert de Lawedre of the Bass於1263年出生在蘇格蘭時，他的父親Robert de Lawedre28歲，母親Elizabeth23歲。他和Lady Elizabeth至少有1個兒子。他去世於1337年在他的家鄉享年74歲。

zh-hans.The Bass Rock, N Berwick, E Lothian, Sco: 苏格兰哈丁頓史爾諾斯 貝維其可
zh-hans.North Berwick, Haddingtonshire, Scotland: 苏格兰哈丁頓史爾諾斯 貝維其可
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert de Lawedre of the Bass于1263年出生在苏格兰时，他的父亲Robert de Lawedre28岁，母亲Elizabeth23岁。他和Lady Elizabeth至少有1个儿子。他去世于1337年在他的家乡享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vietnamese: unexplained.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄭, possibly based on its Hokkien or Teochew pronunciation, see   Zheng   1.

variants.name: Zheng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jian, Yi, Bin, Hong, Hua, Li, Wei, Yong, Ting, Xin, Yan, Hui, Min, Hao, Shen, Da, Quan, Tian, Chong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 鄭: from Zheng (鄭), the name of a state (located in part of present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) granted to You, the younger brother of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). The state of Zheng was annexed by the state of Han (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Bass
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English bas(e), bass, Old French bas(se) ‘low, short’ (from Latin bassus ‘thickset’, i.e. wide as opposed to tall), either a descriptive nickname for a short person or a status name meaning ‘of humble origin’, not necessarily with derogatory connotations.

English: from the Middle English personal name Bas(s)e. This could be an Old French form of ancient Germanic Baso (perhaps meaning ‘purple’) or more commonly a pet form of Middle English and Old French Basile or Basily, used of men and women alike. Compare Basley and Basil .

English: in some instances, from Middle English bærs, bas ‘freshwater perch, bass or any of various related or similar fish, such as the sea bass’, hence a nickname for a person supposedly resembling this fish, or a metonymic occupational name for a fish seller or fisherman.

variants.name: Basley
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from the Middle English personal name Basile or Basily, used for men and women alike. It is a borrowing of Old French Basil(e), from Latin Basilius (see Basil ) and its feminine form Basilia. For a pet form of the name see Bass .

variants.name: Baskin
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Irish Ó Baiscinn, from the personal name Bascaoin meaning ‘fair of hand’. The surname arose from a historical population group called Corca Bhaiscinn ‘seed of Bascaoin’ in west County Clare, and is independent of the Norman name (see 3 below).

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name Baskin, a borrowing of Flemish or Picard Basequin, a pet form of Base (ancient Germanic Baso, perhaps meaning ‘purple’) + the diminutive suffix -kin. It could also have been used as a pet form of Old French Basile. See Bass .

Jewish (from Belarus and Ukraine): metronymic, formed with the Slavic possessive suffix -in, from the Yiddish female personal name Baske, a pet form of the Biblical name Bath Seba under the influence of Polish Basia, a pet form of Barbara.

variants.name: Baskett
meanings: English:

from Middle English basket ‘basket’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a basket maker, or perhaps for someone who carried a basket or pannier as part of his work, for example, carrying baskets of stone to a lime kiln. In some cases it appears to have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a basket (probably owned by a basket maker).
variant of Bascott, a habitational name from Bascote in Warwickshire, probably so named with an unattested Old English personal name Basuca + cot ‘cottage’.

variants.name: Bayes
meanings: English: variant of   Bass   1-3.

variants.name: Base
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Bass  .
German (Bäse): variant of Bese (see Beese ). Compare Baese .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert de Lawedre of the Bass was born in 1263, in Scotland, his father, Robert de Lawedre, was 28 and his mother, Elizabeth, was 23. He had at least 1 son with Lady Elizabeth. He died in 1337, in his hometown, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert de Lawedre of the Bass est né en 1263, à Écosse, son père, Robert de Lawedre, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth, avait 23 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Lady Elizabeth. Il est décédé en 1337, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert de Lawedre of the Bass 1263 in Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Lawedre 28 und seine Mutter Elizabeth 23. Er hatte mit Lady Elizabeth mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1337 im Alter von 74 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert de Lawedre of the Bass è nato il nel 1263 nel Scotland, il suo padre, Robert de Lawedre, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth, aveva 23 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Lady Elizabeth. Morì nel 1337, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Lawedre of the Bassが1263年にスコットランドで生まれた時，父Robert de Lawedreは28歳，母Elizabethは23歳であった。彼はLady Elizabethとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1337年に故郷で74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Lawedre of the Bass(이)가 1263년 스컷랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Lawedre(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Lady Elizabeth(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1337년 그의 고향에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert de Lawedre of the Bass nasceu em 1263, em Scotland, seu pai, Robert de Lawedre, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth, tinha 23 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Lady Elizabeth. Ele faleceu em 1337, em sua cidade natal, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert de Lawedre of the Bass родился в 1263 г. в местности Шотландия, его отцу по имени Robert de Lawedre было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth, 23. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Lady Elizabeth. Он умер в 1337 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Robert de Lawedre of the Bass nació en 1263, en Scotland, su papá, Robert de Lawedre, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Elizabeth, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Lady Elizabeth. Murió en 1337, en su pueblo, a la edad de 74 años.
